headlines.description: Manchester City striker Erling Haaland wasted no time opening his account for the new Premier League season with two goals as the champions began with a 3-0 stroll against promoted Burnley at Turf Moor on Friday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland fires City to opening win over Burnley

headlines.description: Arsenal began their new Premier League campaign with a 2-1 home win over Nottingham Forest on Saturday thanks to first-half goals from Eddie Nketiah and Bukayo Saka.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal beat Forest in Premier League opener

name: West Ham United at AFC Bournemouth

headlines.description: A late equaliser by Dominic Solanke ensured Bournemouth's new manager Andoni Iraola marked his first game in the Premier League with a 1-1 draw at home to West Ham United on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Solanke earns Bournemouth point at home to West Ham

name: Luton Town at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Brighton & Hove Albion spoiled any hopes of a successful start to Luton Town's top-flight return with a comprehensive 4-1 home win over the Hatters in their Premier League clash on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton win ruins Luton's top-flight return

headlines.description: Bobby De Cordova-Reid led a smash and grab for Fulham when he scored with a rare shot on target in their 1-0 Premier League victory over a wasteful Everton at Goodison Park on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: De Cordova-Reid earns Fulham fortunate win at Everton

headlines.description: A second-half strike from Odsonne Edouard was enough to earn Crystal Palace a 1-0 win at Sheffield United, giving the hosts an unhappy return to the Premier League after a two-year absence.

headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur gave new manager Ange Postecoglou a rollercoaster Premier League debut when they started life after Harry Kane with a 2-2 draw in their opening game at Brentford on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs start post-Kane era with draw vs. Brentford

headlines.description: New Chelsea coach Mauricio Pochettino saw the team he rebuilt in the close season fight back to draw 1-1 at home to Liverpool in their Premier League opener on Sunday as debutant defender Axel Disasi cancelled out Luis Diaz's opener.

headlines.description: Manchester United defender Raphael Varane scored a late winner as the hosts survived several scares to claim a 1-0 Premier League win over Wolverhampton Wanderers on Monday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man Utd eke out opening win on late Varane goal

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest substitute Chris Wood headed an 89th minute winner to secure a 2-1 victory over promoted Sheffield United for his side's first three points of the season on Friday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest sub Wood snatches 2-1 win over Sheff Utd

headlines.description: Yoane Wissa and Bryan Mbeumo fired Brentford to a 3-0 win at Fulham in the Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Mbeumo double helps Brentford ease past Fulham

headlines.description: Liverpool survived Alexis Mac Allister's sending off to claim a comeback 3-1 win over Bournemouth in the Premier League at Anfield on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool survive red card to beat Bournemouth

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Wolverhampton Wanderers

headlines.description: Brighton went top of the Premier League table with a clinical 4-1 win at Wolves on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton go top after 4-1 thrashing at Wolves

headlines.description: New Tottenham Hotspur manager Ange Postecoglou celebrated his first home game in charge with a 2-0 victory over Manchester United on Saturday, thanks to Pape Matar Sarr's maiden Premier League goal and a late own goal by Lisandro Martinez.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs kick-start new era with win over Man Utd

headlines.description: A superb strike from Julián Álvarez off a Phil Foden assist gave Manchester City a 1-0 win over Newcastle United in their first home game of the Premier League season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Superb Foden inspires City win over Newcastle

headlines.description: Aston Villa claimed their first three points of the Premier League season with an emphatic 4-0 home win over Everton on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa thrash Everton for 1st win of season

headlines.description: New Chelsea manager Mauricio Pochettino's wait for a first victory goes on as his side were beaten 3-1 away at West Ham United on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea winless after loss to 10-man West Ham

headlines.description: Arsenal captain Martin Odegaard's penalty secured a 1-0 win over Crystal Palace in the Premier League at Selhurst Park on Monday.
headlines.shortLinkText: 10-man Arsenal hang on for 1-0 win at Palace

headlines.shortLinkText: Sterling double gives Pochettino first Chelsea win

headlines.shortLinkText: Nunez seals last-gasp win for 10-man Liverpool

name: West Ham United at Luton Town

headlines.shortLinkText: Late Mbeumo goal earns Brentford 2-2 home draw with Bournemouth

name: Newcastle United at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton crush Newcastle with Ferguson hat-trick

name: AFC Bournemouth at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford beat Burnley 3-0 to end losing streak

headlines.description: Newcastle United striker Alexander Isak netted on his return from injury, while two quick-fire second-half goals and a late Anthony Gordon strike gave the Magpies a 4-1 Premier League win over 10-man Chelsea at St. James' Park on Saturday.

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Nottingham Forest

headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton beat Forest 3-2 despite Lewis Dunk red card

headlines.description: Winger Marcus Tavernier netted twice as Bournemouth put in an assured performance to beat sluggish Sheffield United 3-1.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bournemouth beat struggling Sheffield United 3-1

headlines.description: Arsenal moved top of the Premier League when a late goal by Kai Havertz earned them a 1-0 victory at Brentford on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Havertz sends Arsenal top with winner at Brentford

headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur slipped to a third successive Premier League defeat as they were beaten 2-1 at home by Aston Villa in an absorbing clash full of chances on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa hand Tottenham third straight loss

headlines.shortLinkText: Garnacho scores stunner in Man Utd win at Everton

headlines.description: Willian's 94th-minute penalty, his second of the game, earned Fulham a dramatic 3-2 win over Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League on Monday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Willian pen double sees Fulham overcome Wolves

headlines.description: Arsenal moved four points clear at the top of the Premier League as goals by Bukayo Saka and Martin Ødegaard fired them to a 2-1 victory over Wolves.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal beat Wolves 2-1 to go four points clear

headlines.description: Brentford moved into the top half of the Premier League table after beating Luton Town 3-1 at the Gtech Community Stadium on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford beat Luton 3-1 to surge into top half

headlines.description: Burnley produced a dazzling display to crush 10-man Sheffield United 5-0 at Turf Moor on Saturday and secure their first home points of the Premier League season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Burnley off bottom after Sheffield United romp

headlines.description: Dwight McNeil scored his first goal of the season to give Everton a precious three points with a 1-0 away win at Nottingham Forest.
headlines.shortLinkText: McNeil strike takes Everton off bottom of PL

headlines.description: Anthony Gordon scored the winner as Newcastle held off a frenzied late push by Manchester United in a 1-0 Premier League victory over their top-four rivals on a chilly night at St James' Park on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle beat Man Utd but lose Pope to injury

headlines.description: A late goal from Ollie Watkins grabbed a point for Aston Villa in a 2-2 draw at Bournemouth after a pulsating, end-to-end Premier League clash on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa leave it late in 2-2 draw with Bournemouth

headlines.description: First Chelsea Premier League goals from Enzo Fernandez and Levi Colwill earned the 10-man home side a 3-2 win over Brighton at a rainy Stamford Bridge on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: 10-man Chelsea stave off Brighton for 3-2 win

headlines.description: Liverpool scored two late goals a minute apart, including Trent Alexander-Arnold's winner in the 88th minute in their 4-3 victory over Fulham on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Liverpool goals complete dramatic 4-3 win

name: Crystal Palace at West Ham United

headlines.description: West Ham United scored early but were held to a 1-1 draw at home by Crystal Palace in the Premier League on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham held to draw at home by Palace

headlines.description: Tottenham's Dejan Kulusevski headed a late equaliser to earn his side a 3-3 draw at champions Manchester City in a rip-roaring Premier League contest on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man City, Spurs share points in thrilling 3-3 draw

headlines.description: Hwang Hee-chan took advantage of a defensive blunder to earn Wolverhampton Wanderers a 1-0 victory over struggling Burnley in their Premier League clash at Molineux on Tuesday that left the visitors rooted in the relegation zone.
headlines.shortLinkText: Burnley blunder gifts Wolves win with Hwang goal

headlines.description: Declan Rice's header deep in stoppage time sealed an enthralling 4-3 victory for Arsenal over Luton Town to send them five points clear at the top of the Premier League on Tuesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rice saves Arsenal with late 4-3 winner at Luton

headlines.description: Jack Hinshelwood saved on the line and then headed a winning first Premier League goal for Brighton & Hove Albion as the south-coast side came from a goal down to beat visitors Brentford 2-1 on Wednesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Hinshelwood gives Brighton win over Brentford

headlines.description: Resurgent Bournemouth climbed further away from the Premier League relegation zone with a comfortable 2-0 victory at Crystal Palace courtesy of goals by Marcos Senesi and Kieffer Moore on Wednesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bournemouth's resurgence continues with win at Palace

headlines.description: Fulham mowed down Nottingham Forest in a 5-0 win on a freezing Wednesday night in west London, dropping the visitors closer to the Premier League relegation zone and piling pressure on their manager Steve Cooper.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fulham thrash Forest with Jiménez, Iwobi braces

headlines.description: Liverpool's Virgil van Dijk scored a first-half volley to put his side on course for a 2-0 Premier League victory over Sheffield United on Wednesday to keep up with leaders Arsenal, who won on Tuesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool win to keep pace with 1st place Arsenal

headlines.description: Aston Villa stunned Manchester City with a fully-deserved 1-0 win on Wednesday thanks to a deflected shot from Leon Bailey, leaving the reigning Premier League champions in an unfamiliar fourth position and six points off the top.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa leapfrog Man City as Bailey inspires big win

headlines.description: Manchester United's Scott McTominay scored either side of halftime to lift his team to a well-deserved 2-1 Premier League victory over Chelsea at Old Trafford on Wednesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: McTominay double sees Man Utd beat Chelsea

headlines.description: Everton profited from two Kieran Trippier errors to score late goals through Dwight McNeil, Abdoulaye Doucoure and Beto as they claimed a 3-0 victory over Newcastle United at Goodison Park on Thursday to move out of the Premier League relegation zone.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton out of drop zone after beating Newcastle

name: West Ham United at Tottenham Hotspur

headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur's inability to convert leads into Premier League points returned with a vengeance in a 2-1 home defeat by West Ham United as their winless run extended to five matches on Thursday.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham fight back to continue Spurs slump

headlines.description: Mohamed Salah netted his 200th goal for Liverpool and Harvey Elliott scored a stoppage-time winner as Liverpool came from behind to beat Crystal Palace 2-1 and go top of the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Salah scores 200th Liverpool goal in dramatic win

headlines.description: Manchester United were humiliated 3-0 at home by Bournemouth on Saturday as the pressure ratcheted up once again on manager Erik Ten Hag.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man United slump to 3-0 Bournemouth defeat

headlines.description: James McAtee scored his first Premier League goal with a stunning strike that gave Sheffield United a 1-0 win over Brentford on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sheffield United beat Brentford to earn rare win

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest earned a point in a 1-1 draw at Wolves as their fans once again showed their support for under-pressure manager Steve Cooper.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cooper's Forest held to 1-1 draw at Wolves

headlines.description: John McGinn's early goal led surging Aston Villa to a club-record 15th consecutive home Premier League victory on Saturday, a breathless 1-0 win over Arsenal that prevented Mikel Arteta's Gunners from reclaiming top spot in the table.
headlines.shortLinkText: In-form Villa beat Arsenal to make club history

headlines.description: Abdoulaye Doucoure scored in his 100th Premier League appearance for Everton and Lewis Dobbin scored late to earn a 2-0 win over Chelsea on Sunday
headlines.shortLinkText: In-form Everton beat struggling Chelsea 2-0

headlines.shortLinkText: Fulham beat West Ham in second straight 5-0 win

headlines.description: Bernardo Silva and Jack Grealish scored three minutes apart as Manchester City came from behind to beat Premier League newcomers Luton Town on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man City come from behind to beat Luton

headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs end winless run with 4-1 Newcastle win

headlines.description: Chelsea scored twice in seven second-half minutes to claim victory over the Premier League's bottom club Sheffield United.

headlines.shortLinkText: Palace stun Man City as Prem slump continues

headlines.description: Everton continued their climb back up the Premier League table after their 10-point deduction with a well-earned 2-0 victory over Burnley at Turf Moor on Saturday, their fifth win in six games.

headlines.description: A dominant Arsenal side beat Brighton & Hove Albion 2-0 at home on Sunday with second-half goals from Gabriel Jesus and Kai Havertz.

headlines.description: Ollie Watkins scored a late winner against his former club as Aston Villa came from behind to win 2-1 away at 10-man Brentford on Sunday.

name: Wolverhampton Wanderers at West Ham United

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Crystal Palace

headlines.shortLinkText: Welbeck recuses Brighton draw at rivals Palace

headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur ended Everton's excellent Premier League run with a 2-1 home victory on Saturday that took them into the top four.

headlines.description: Bournemouth moved into the top half of the Premier League table with a 3-0 home win over Fulham on Tuesday

headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool beat Burnley to go top of the table

headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur brought resurgent Bournemouth down to earth with a flattering 3-1  Premier League victory thanks to goals by Pape Matar Sarr, Son Heung-Min and Richarlison on Sunday.

headlines.description: Mohamed Salah's second-half brace made up for a missed penalty as Liverpool kicked off the new year three points clear atop the Premier League standings with a 4-2 victory over Newcastle United at a rain-drenched Anfield on Monday.

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at West Ham United

headlines.description: Liverpool moved five points clear at the top of the Premier League with a 4-0 demolition of Bournemouth

name: Wolverhampton Wanderers at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton leapfrog Man United with Wolves draw

headlines.description: Gabriel Jesus scored and then set up Bukayo Saka for another seven minutes later to lead Arsenal to a 2-1 Premier League win over Nottingham Forest at the City Ground on Tuesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal hang on to earn narrow win over Forest

headlines.description: Struggling duo Fulham and Everton fought out a 0-0 Premier League draw at Craven Cottage on Tuesday, with the visitors lucky to escape with a potentially precious point after being under sustained pressure in an all-action second half.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton left in bottom 3 after draw with Fulham

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Luton Town

headlines.description: Luton Town thrashed Brighton & Hove Albion 4-0 to move out of the Premier League relegation zone with Elijah Adebayo scoring a hat-trick after the Hatters went two goals up inside the first three minutes at Kenilworth Road on Tuesday.

headlines.shortLinkText: Olise inspires Palace to 3-2 win over Sheff Utd

headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle end Villa's long unbeaten home run

headlines.shortLinkText: Birthday boy Álvarez scores brace in City win

headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur climbed to fourth place in the Premier League as a second-half broadside fired them to a 3-2 home victory against struggling Brentford on Wednesday.

headlines.description: Liverpool moved five points clear at the top of the Premier League table with a 4-1 thrashing of Chelsea on Wednesday as Darwin Núñez struck the woodwork four times.

headlines.description: West Ham United debutant Kalvin Phillips gifted Bournemouth an early goal before the Hammers fought back to earn a 1-1 Premier League draw at the London Stadium on Thursday.

name: Crystal Palace at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Brighton ended a run of three Premier League games without a win with a 4-1 home victory over Crystal Palace on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton put four past rivals Palace in derby win

headlines.description: David Datro Fofana scored twice in the second half to earn relegation-threatened Burnley a dramatic 2-2 draw with Fulham in the Premier League.

headlines.shortLinkText: Man Utd's young stars inspire win over West Ham

headlines.shortLinkText: Alisson errors see Arsenal defeat Liverpool

headlines.description: Phil Foden scored a brilliantly composed hat-trick as Manchester City fought back to earn a 3-1 win at Brentford on Monday and close to within two points of Premier League leaders Liverpool with a game in hand.
headlines.shortLinkText: Manchester City rally to close gap on Liverpool

headlines.shortLinkText: Late Ritchie goal gives Newcastle 2-2 draw with Bournemouth

name: West Ham United at Nottingham Forest

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest climbed five points clear of the relegation zone with a 2-0 win over West Ham on Saturday, as stoppage-time goals in both halves by Taiwo Awoniyi and Callum Hudson-Odoi earned them their first Premier League win since December.

headlines.shortLinkText: Gomes double fires Wolves to win at Tottenham

headlines.description: Rodri's late goal salvaged a 1-1 Premier League draw for Manchester City against Chelsea on Saturday as the champions avoided their first home loss in 15 months but had their 12-game winning streak across all competitions ended.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rodri rescues draw as Chelsea frustrate Man City

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Sheffield United

headlines.description: A late Simon Adingra double secured Brighton & Hove Albion's biggest away win in the Premier League as they won 5-0 at bottom side Sheffield United.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton blitz hapless Sheff Utd in 5-goal rout

headlines.description: Alex Iwobi scored deep in stoppage time as Fulham shocked sub-par Manchester United 2-1 in the Premier League on Saturday.

headlines.shortLinkText: Utd lose 4-0 at Palace for record 13th Prem defeat

headlines.description: Manchester City hit the top of the Premier League with one week of the season left as two goals by Josko Gvardiol and one each from Phil Foden and Julian Alvarez sealed an emphatic 4-0 victory at Fulham on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gvardiol double sends Man City top of the table

headlines.description: Yoane Wissa scored four minutes into added time to secure a 2-1 Premier League win for Brentford at Bournemouth on Saturday after a flurry of late goals.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wissa strikes late as Brentford beat Bournemouth

headlines.description: Abdoulaye Doucoure scored a first-half winner as Everton beat relegated Sheffield United 1-0 at a balmy Goodison Park on Saturday, setting one new club Premier League record and inflicting another unwanted one on their opponents.
headlines.shortLinkText: Doucoure sets Everton record with Sheff Utd win

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Newcastle United

headlines.description: Sean Longstaff's equaliser in first-half stoppage time earned Newcastle United a 1-1 draw with Brighton & Hove Albion on Saturday in the Magpies' final home game of a see-saw Premier League season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle held but European spot still in sight

headlines.description: Burnley were relegated from the Premier League after a 2-1 defeat at Tottenham Hotspur on Saturday left Vincent Kompany's side five points from the safety zone with one game remaining.
headlines.shortLinkText: Burnley relegated from PL following Spurs loss

name: Luton Town at West Ham United

headlines.description: A fine second half performance saw West Ham earn a 3-1 win against struggling Luton Town.
headlines.shortLinkText: Luton on brink of relegation after West Ham loss

headlines.description: Strikes from Michael Olise, Jean-Philippe Mateta and Eberechi Eze earned Crystal Palace a 3-1 win over Wolverhampton Wanderers on Saturday, with the London club securing their fifth victory in six Premier League games.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palace into 12th after dominant win at Wolves

headlines.description: Late goals from Raheem Sterling and Nicolas Jackson lifted Chelsea to a 3-2 Premier League victory at Nottingham Forest on Saturday that left the hosts mathematically still in danger of relegation.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea stage late comeback to beat Forest 3-2

headlines.description: Arsenal's Leandro Trossard capitalised on dreadful Manchester United defending to give his side a 1-0 victory on Sunday that saw Mikel Arteta's men provisionally overtake Manchester City for top spot in the Premier League table.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal edge past United to maintain title shot

headlines.description: Substitute Jhon Durán scored two late goals as Aston Villa fought back to salvage a 3-3 home draw with Liverpool on Monday and take a significant step towards a place in the Champions League next season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa snatch Liverpool draw on late Durán double

headlines.description: Erling Haaland's double put Manchester City within touching distance of an unprecedented fourth successive Premier League title as they beat Tottenham Hotspur 2-0 away on Tuesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man City beat Spurs, on brink of record Prem title

headlines.description: Cole Palmer and Christopher Nkunku struck either side of halftime to earn Chelsea a 2-1 win over Brighton & Hove Albion on Wednesday, sending the London club into the final day of the Premier League season in sixth place.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea beat Brighton to bolster Europa chances

headlines.description: Amad scored his first Premier League goal and assisted on another to power Manchester United to a 3-2 victory over Newcastle United on Wednesday at Old Trafford in a battle between teams fighting for a European berth next season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man Utd beat Newcastle to boost European hopes

headlines.description: Arsenal's 2-1 victory over Everton proved in vain as their hopes of a first Premier League title for 20 years were crushed on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal defeat Everton; finish second in PL

headlines.description: Bruno Guimarães starred as Newcastle United won 4-2 at Brentford in the Premier League on Sunday to potentially qualify for Europe next season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle win 4-2 to keep European hopes alive

name: Manchester United at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Diogo Dalot and Rasmus Hojlund gave Manchester United a scarcely-deserved 2-0 win at Brighton & Hove Albion on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man Utd limp to win at Brighton on PL final day

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest striker Chris Wood scored two goals to secure a 2-1 victory at relegated Burnley as his side finished their Premier League season with a flourish at Turf Moor on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wood double confirms Nottingham Forest survival

headlines.description: A magnificent lobbed goal by Moises Caicedo and a twisting effort from Raheem Sterling earned Chelsea a 2-1 win over Bournemouth on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea beat Bournemouth to clinch European place

headlines.description: A hat trick by Jean-Philippe Mateta and Eberechi Eze double saw Crystal Palace thrash Aston Villa 5-0 in the Premier League on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Mateta scores hat-trick as Palace thrash Villa 5-0

headlines.description: Liverpool delivered much-loved manager Jürgen Klopp a victory in his final game with the club on Sunday beating Wolverhampton Wanderers 2-0.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool beat Wolves in emotional Klopp farewell

headlines.description: Raul Jimenez scored twice as Fulham won 4-2 at Luton Town on Sunday to confirm their hosts' relegation from the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Luton's relegation confirmed as Fulham win 4-2

name: West Ham United at Manchester City

headlines.description: Manchester City won a fourth straight English top-flight title with a 3-1 win over West Ham on Sunday to pip Arsenal on the final day of the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Manchester City wins record 4th straight PL title

headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur beat Sheffield United 3-0 away at Bramall Lane in their final Premier League game of the campaign on Sunday to qualify for the Europa League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs beat Sheff Utd, earn Europa League spot
